perturbation.original_text: Bach Party hereto agrees that this Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.changed_text: Bach Party hereto agrees that only the financial information of the Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.explanation: This change limits the scope of 'Proprietary Information' that's considered 'commercial and financial information' to only the 'financial information,' while earlier it said it was 'commercial and financial information'. This raises questions on whether the commercial information from the Proprietary information is also to be considered to be treated with the same strict rules.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause only its directors and officers (collectively "Affiliates") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement. Bach Party hereto agrees that only the financial information of the Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process. Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement (except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the seine confidential) without the prior written consent of the other Patty, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information.' The changed text narrows this to 'only the financial information of the Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information.' This creates a contradiction with the section 'Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause only its directors and officers (collectively "Affiliates") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement,' because it is unclear whether the 'business affairs, property, or methods of operation' that are not financial information are still considered Proprietary Information that must be kept confidential.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Inform on shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information or its Affiliates; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party; or (c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Inform on shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party; or (c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or its Affiliates' from exception (a), information that becomes public through the receiving party's affiliates is now considered Proprietary Information. This creates a contradiction with section 1, where affiliates were initially required to maintain confidentiality and raises issues on who's reponsibility and actions are now considered Proprietary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause only its directors and officers (collectively "Affiliates") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement. Bach Party hereto agrees that only the financial information of the Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.justification: The change removes 'or its Affiliates' from the list of entities that could cause information to enter the public domain without violating confidentiality. This means that if an affiliate of the receiving party releases Proprietary Information into the public domain, it could now be considered Proprietary Information, contradicting the Contradicted Text, which requires Affiliates to maintain confidentiality of Proprietary Information. This creates a direct conflict because the affiliate's actions could simultaneously make information public and still be considered a breach of confidentiality under the contract, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that any Proprietary Information given by the disclosing Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party, including all applicable rights to patents, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets inherent therein and appurtenant thereto, and that no license or other right therein are created by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that any Proprietary Information given by the disclosing Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party, excluding all applicable rights to trade secrets inherent therein and appurtenant thereto, and that no license or other right therein are created by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies section 3 to exclude trade secrets from the proprietary information considered the 'sole and exclusive property' of the disclosing party. This creates an inconsistency as the agreement may discuss trade secrets, but those trade secrets are not protected with the same level as other Proprietary information. Meaning that now only the trade secrets doesn't fall under the Agreement terms, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause only its directors and officers (collectively "Affiliates") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement. Bach Party hereto agrees that only the financial information of the Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'trade secrets' from the definition of 'Proprietary Information' that is considered the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing party. The contradicted text obligates each party to maintain the confidentiality of 'any Proprietary Information'. By excluding trade secrets from the definition in the changed text, the receiving party no longer has an explicit obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the disclosing party's trade secrets, creating a direct conflict and undermining the purpose of the confidentiality clause. This means the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a Transaction between Parties is not entered into, or is entered into but thereafter terminated, each Party will, at the request of the other Party, promptly deliver all Proprietary Information to the other Party without retaining any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a Transaction between Parties is not entered into, or is entered into but thereafter terminated, each Party will, at the request of the other Party, promptly deliver all Proprietary Information to the other Party with the option to retaining one (1) copy thereof for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces the option for retaining a copy of Proprietary Information after termination, contradicting the initial requirement to return all such information. This is contradiction as it is not defining how long this copy will be hold, while the agreement ends at 5 years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from date hereof. In the event any court shall determine that the length of time or the areas coveted by any one individual stipulation, term, or provision excessive, the Agreement shall nut be rendered invalid thereby and the court shall modify the length of time or the area covered to the extent necessary to make the Agreement valid and binding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the option to retain a copy of Proprietary Information, while the contradicted text states that the agreement is only valid for 5 years. This creates a contradiction because the changed text does not specify how long the copy can be retained for, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The changed clause alters the initial absolute requirement to return all Proprietary Information, which directly breaks compatibility with the agreement's 5-year duration stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties of THIS Agreement acknowledge that no effort shall be made to circumvent its terms in an attempt to gain cessions, fees, remuneration or considerations to the benefit of any of the Parties of this Agreement while excluding equal or agreed to benefits to any of the other parties. And that further, throughout the terns OF this agreement the parties agree that they shall not, as either owner, co-owner, partner, joint venture, employees, agent, sales person, service person, officer, director, stockholder, or in any other capacity whatsoever, or on behalf of any other business entity, become engaged directly or indirectly with any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement without the direct participation and authority of-the introducing party. The parties agree that any such breech of this provision shall cause severe damage for which the injured party will have the right to consequential monetary damages and injunctive relief and protection against the offending party and any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement with whom the offending party enters into such circumvention.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties of THIS Agreement acknowledge that no effort shall be made to circumvent its terms in an attempt to gain cessions, fees, remuneration or considerations to the benefit of any of the Parties of this Agreement while excluding equal or agreed to benefits to any of the other parties. And that further, throughout the terns OF this agreement the parties agree that they shall not, as either owner, co-owner, partner, joint venture, employees, agent, sales person, service person, officer, director, stockholder, or in any other capacity whatsoever, or on behalf of any other business entity, become engaged directly with any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement without the direct participation and authority of-the introducing party. The parties agree that any such breech of this provision shall only cause nominal damage for which the injured party will have the right to consequential monetary damages and injunctive relief and protection against the offending party and any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement with whom the offending party enters into such circumvention.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'severe damage' with 'nominal damage,' this significantly reduces the consequences of breaching the non-circumvention clause, creating a contradiction if other parts of the agreement emphasize the importance of this clause. This is contradictory because now it is considering 'nominal damage' when Proprietary Infomation is being discussed and the implications it has, in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands That the Proprietary Information disclosed to it is a unique and valuable asset of the other Party, that violation of this Agreement would cause the other Party immediate and irreparable harm, and that the remedies at law may be inadequate. Each Party therefore consents to the entry of a restraining order and a preliminary and permanent injunction restraining any such violation without proof of actual damages. Each Party agrees that any money damages shall include all pecuniary beets obtained by the receiving Party and its Affiliates as a result of any brunch of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a breach of the non-circumvention provision would cause 'severe damage,' allowing for consequential monetary damages and injunctive relief. The changed text modifies this to 'nominal damage,' while retaining the remedies of consequential monetary damages and injunctive relief. The contradicted text emphasizes that the Proprietary Information disclosed is a unique and valuable asset and that any violation would cause immediate and irreparable harm, making remedies at law inadequate. By changing 'severe damage' to 'nominal damage,' the modified text now contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text makes it seem like breaching the non-circumvention clause related to proprietary information is only a minor issue ('nominal damage'), directly conflicting with the contradicted text's assertion that such a violation would cause 'immediate and irreparable harm' and require remedies beyond monetary damages. This creates a clear and direct in-text contradiction, fitting the definition of ambiguities that lead to in-text contradictions, as key obligations are now defined inconsistently.
